title: Uptake/operator boundary candidate
notes: Eleventh-round mutation generated after clause-type and interjection cards exposed a boundary OPG could not represent: uptake configuration alone is not operator-repertoire membership. This graph separates update-role configuration, repertoire closedness, token innovability, operator-repertoire membership, genre/register fit, social-indexical value, recoverability, repair pressure, acceptability, and attribution.

edges.rationale: Update-role configuration is warrant for possible operator-repertoire membership, but is not sufficient by itself.
A closed, mutually accountable contrast set is warrant for operator-repertoire membership.
High stretchability, repeatability, blending, or nonce substitution undercuts treating the form as a closed operator-repertoire value.
Operator value can be part of operator-repertoire membership, but the boundary also requires update-role and repertoire-closure evidence.
Membership in a closed update-role repertoire is contextual warrant for licensing in that repertoire.
Operator-repertoire membership provides warrant that a mismatch is grammar-relevant rather than merely stance- or content-relevant.
Lower licensing in the relevant repertoire predicts greater repair or reformulation pressure.
A misconfigured uptake role can prompt repair, clarification, or response-space correction depending on the activity.
Genre conditions whether a clause type, minor clause, exclamation, or interjection token is appropriate.
Medium affects how uptake-configuring forms are interpreted and policed.
Higher register or genre fit tends to increase reported acceptability for boundary forms.
Speaker or persona cues can change the social value assigned to an interjection or exclamation.
Stance and persona can shift reported acceptability without creating operator-repertoire membership.
Task framing can foreground grammaticality, uptake, register, stance, or comprehension.
Task framing can shift explicit attribution by cueing whether the problem is grammar, stance, uptake, or recognizability.
Recoverable content or stance can raise reported acceptability without proving operator-repertoire membership.
Licensing in the relevant community or repertoire supports production availability.
Acceptability can measure licensing, but boundary cases are especially task-biased.
Repair pressure is contextual warrant for attribution, but can target uptake, stance, or grammar depending on the case.
Acceptability reports provide contextual warrant for attribution without defining grammatical status.

score_status.scope: Boundary module for clause type, uptake role, interjection-like forms, repertoire closedness, token innovability, stance, repair, and attribution; not a general grammaticality account.
